raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need to copy all files except those containing 'text' in name? Probably exclude files with 'text' in name. Use rsync or find. Command: mkdir -p target then find /system -type f ! -name '*text*' -exec cp --parents {} /system/folder3/backup_dbg/backup \; But need preserve directory structure? Not specified. Just copy all files. Use cp -r? Let's use rsync: rsync -av --exclude='*text*' /system/ /system/folder3/backup_dbg/backup/ . That copies all files and dirs except those containing text. But exclude only files? It will exclude directories too. Maybe fine. Provide command.
